EMAIL.description: The email field must declare the 'required' attribute if submission without a value should be blocked. A primary email field without 'required' allows empty submissions that the server must then handle.
The field should use type='email' for mobile keyboard optimization and basic browser validation.
The field must declare an 'id' attribute to be accessible and programmatically labelable.
The field must declare a 'name' attribute for form submission and server-side processing.
The field should have an aria-label or associated label element for screen reader accessibility.
The email field should declare a maxlength attribute to prevent excessive input (RFC 5321 max: 254).
The field must not accept plain text without '@' under any submit context.
Verify rejection of email missing domain part (user@ is invalid).
Verify rejection of email missing local part (@domain.com is invalid).
Test rejection of emails with unquoted backslash in local part (RFC 5321 invalid unquoted character).
The field must reject email addresses containing more than one '@' symbol.
Verify rejection of email with dot at start of local part (.user@test.com is invalid per RFC 5321). HTML5 does not block this format — test via blur for DOM error feedback.
Verify rejection of email with dot at end of local part (user.@test.com is invalid per RFC 5321). HTML5 does not block this format — test via blur for DOM error feedback.
Verify rejection of email with consecutive dots in local part (user..name@test.com). HTML5 does not block this format — test via blur for DOM error feedback.
Verify system accepts emails at local part boundary (64 chars = RFC 5321 max).
Verify rejection of email exceeding local part boundary (65 chars > RFC 5321 max of 64).
Verify system accepts email at total length boundary (254 chars = RFC 5321 max).
Verify rejection of email exceeding RFC 5321 max total length (255 chars).
Verify rejection of email with domain without TLD dot (user@test is invalid).
Verify rejection of email with hyphen at start of domain label (RFC 5321 invalid). HTML5 does not block this format — test via blur for DOM error feedback.
Verify rejection of email with hyphen at end of domain label (RFC 5321 invalid). HTML5 does not block this format — test via blur for DOM error feedback.
Verify rejection of email with consecutive dots in domain part (user@test..com is invalid).
Verify rejection of email containing unquoted spaces in the local part (user @test.com is invalid).

Verify the field does not silently accept a leading space: ' user@test.com' is not a valid email. The field must block submission or show an error — silent acceptance creates inconsistent login credentials.

Verify the field does not silently accept a trailing space: 'user@test.com ' is not a valid email. The field must block submission or show an error — silent acceptance creates inconsistent login credentials.

Verify rejection of email containing control characters (e.g. null bytes, ASCII 0x01). These must be blocked at the frontend or rejected by the server — passing them to the backend is a security risk.
Verify that SQL injection patterns in the email field are blocked by the frontend (invalid email format) or rejected by the server with a 4xx response.
Verify that XSS payloads in the email field are blocked by the frontend (invalid email format) or rejected by the server with a 4xx response.
Verify that a well-formed valid email address is accepted by the form without friction.

Verify rejection of email containing a zero-width space (U+200B) in the local part. Zero-width spaces are invisible to users but corrupt the email address — they must be blocked or stripped with explicit feedback.
Verify acceptance of email with all-numeric TLD (user@test.123). Per RFC 5321 and ICANN, numeric TLDs are technically valid — a system that rejects them is over-restrictive.

PASSWORD.description: Passwords must use type='password' to mask characters on screen.
Login password fields must declare the 'required' attribute — an unprotected empty submission is a Critical vulnerability in authentication forms.
The password field should have an accessible label for screen readers.
The password field should declare autocomplete='current-password' or 'new-password' for credential managers.
Verify that passwords below minimum length (typically 8 chars — payload: 7 chars) are rejected by the form.
Verify that a password at exactly the minimum valid length (8 chars) is accepted without friction.
Verify that extremely short passwords (3 chars) are definitively rejected.
Verify that an empty string is rejected as a password — empty submissions must be blocked.
Verify that a password consisting only of spaces is rejected or trimmed-then-rejected. HTML5 does not block whitespace-only input — test via blur for DOM error feedback.
Verify that passwords with internal spaces are accepted — spaces are valid password characters and must not be silently stripped.
Verify acceptance of passwords without numbers — complexity enforcement (requiring numbers) is only expected in registration/change-password forms, not login.
Verify acceptance of passwords without special characters — complexity enforcement is only expected in registration/change-password forms, not login.
Verify acceptance of passwords without uppercase letters — complexity enforcement is only expected in registration/change-password forms, not login.
Verify that passwords with special characters are accepted — blocking special chars reduces password entropy and is an unjustified constraint.
Verify that passwords with extended Latin characters (accented, e.g. Árbol) are accepted.
Verify that passwords with Unicode characters (e.g. CJK) are accepted or consistently handled — the system must not crash or corrupt the value.
Verify system accepts password at the typical max boundary (128 chars).

If a maxlength attribute is declared on the password field, the server must enforce the same constraint. A declared maxlength not enforced server-side is a bypassable control — Playwright's fill() bypasses HTML maxlength, so overflow payloads can reach the server regardless of the attribute.

Verify that a password exceeding the declared maxlength (128 chars) is blocked or rejected. If the overflow payload reaches the server and is accepted, the maxlength constraint is bypassable — an incongruency between declared and enforced limits.

Verify that SQL injection payloads in the password field are rejected by the server (4xx response). Unlike email, password fields have no HTML5 format validation — the request will reach the server and it must reject it.

Verify that XSS payloads in the password field are rejected by the server (4xx response). Unlike email, password fields have no HTML5 format validation — the request will reach the server and it must reject it.

Verify the field gives feedback when a leading space is entered in a password. Silent acceptance is dangerous: the user's effective password differs from what they believe they typed, causing silent authentication failures.

Verify the field gives feedback when a trailing space is entered in a password. Silent acceptance is dangerous: the user's effective password differs from what they believe they typed, causing silent authentication failures.
Verify a typical valid password (mixed case + number, e.g. Password1) is accepted without friction.

Verify rejection of a password containing a zero-width space (U+200B). Zero-width spaces are invisible to users but alter the actual password value — they must be rejected or stripped with explicit feedback to prevent silent credential corruption.

The password field should declare a minlength attribute to communicate the minimum requirement to the browser and screen readers. Absence of minlength means the browser cannot assist users before submission.

TEXT.description: Mandatory text fields must have the required attribute and block submission when empty.
Text fields must declare an 'id' attribute for accessibility and programmatic access.
Text fields must declare a 'name' attribute for form submission.
Text fields should be associated with a label for screen reader accessibility.
Text fields should declare a maxlength attribute to prevent excessive input.
Verify that empty text fields block submission when required.
Verify rejection of input containing only whitespace characters.
Verify consistent handling of leading spaces in text fields. The field may trim or accept them; if trimmed silently without feedback, data integrity issues may occur.
Verify consistent handling of trailing spaces in text fields. The field may trim or accept them; if trimmed silently without feedback, data integrity issues may occur.
Verify standard alphanumeric text is accepted without error.
Verify text with extended Latin characters (accents, tildes) is accepted.
Verify text with Unicode characters (CJK, etc.) is handled consistently.
Verify text with special characters is accepted unless there's a business rule against it.
Test system response to 500-character text input (overflow detection).
Test system response to 5000-character destructive overflow.
Verify the field rejects or escapes HTML injection attempts.
Test for Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability in text field.
Test for SQL Injection vulnerability in text field.
Verify rejection of text containing control characters (null bytes, etc.).
Verify consistent handling of multiple consecutive internal spaces.
If a pattern attribute is declared, verify it matches the expected field semantics.
If minlength is declared, verify its value is reasonable (> 0 and < maxlength).
Detect incoherence: a field cannot be both required and disabled simultaneously.

NUMBER.description: Numeric fields should use type='number' or type='tel' for keyboard optimization.
Mandatory numeric fields must have the required attribute set.
Numeric fields should be associated with an accessible label.
Numeric fields should declare a min attribute if there's a lower bound constraint.
Numeric fields should declare a max attribute if there's an upper bound constraint.
Verify that empty numeric fields block submission when required.
Verify that purely alphabetical input is rejected in numeric fields.
Verify that mixed alphanumeric input is rejected in numeric fields.
Verify that special characters are rejected in numeric fields.
Verify consistent handling of zero value input.

Test system behavior on negative value input. A numeric field that is expected to only accept positive values should reject negatives; fields that legitimately accept negatives (balances, temperatures, coordinates) should allow them.
Verify that a standard positive integer is accepted.
Verify consistent behavior with decimal (float) values.
Verify rejection of extremely large numbers that exceed typical integer limits.
Verify consistent handling of scientific notation (1e5 = 100000). Some browsers accept this in type=number fields; if the business rule requires integers only, scientific notation must be rejected.
Verify consistent handling of negative zero value (-0).
Verify the field does not silently accept leading-space numbers.
Verify the field does not silently accept trailing-space numbers.
Verify the field rejects HTML injection attempts.
Test for SQL Injection vulnerability in numeric field.
If a step attribute is declared, verify it enforces correct value increments.
Detect incoherence: a numeric field cannot be both required and disabled simultaneously.
Test for Cross-Site Scripting vulnerability in numeric field.
Numeric fields must declare a 'name' attribute for form submission.
If a placeholder is declared, verify it indicates a numeric format.
If both min and max are declared, verify that min < max to avoid illogical constraints.
Detect incoherence: a required field that is also readonly may prevent the user from entering a value.
Insert destructive overflow string to test server-side numeric type casting vulnerabilities.
Verify rejection of malformed floats with multiple decimal points (1.1.1). These are invalid numbers that some parsers may mishandle, causing type confusion or crashes.
Verify rejection or safe handling of large negative overflow values (-9999999999999). These can trigger signed integer overflow or incorrect type casting on the server.

PHONE.description: Phone number fields marked as required must have the attribute and block empty submissions.
Phone fields should use type='tel' for numeric keyboard on mobile devices.
Phone fields should have an accessible label for screen reader support.
Phone fields must declare a 'name' attribute for form submission.
Phone fields should declare a pattern attribute to enforce valid format.
Verify that empty phone fields block submission when required.
Verify rejection of alphabetical characters in phone number field.
Verify rejection of mixed alphanumeric input in phone number field.
Verify rejection of extremely short phone number inputs.
Verify rejection of phone numbers exceeding typical maximum length (15 digits per E.164).
Verify that a standard 10-digit number is accepted.
Verify consistent handling of international prefix (+52...) in phone fields.
Verify rejection of multiple '+' prefixes in phone number (++52...).
Verify rejection of '+' symbol appearing in the middle of a phone number.
Verify consistent handling of dash-separated phone format (55-1234-5678).
Verify consistent handling of space-separated phone format (55 1234 5678).
Verify consistent handling of parentheses in phone format ((55)12345678).
Verify rejection of special characters (@#!) in phone number field.
Verify consistent handling of phone extension notation.
Verify rejection of phone numbers containing control characters.
Verify the phone field rejects HTML injection attempts.
Test for SQL Injection vulnerability in phone number field.
Test for Cross-Site Scripting vulnerability in phone number field.
Detect incoherence: a phone field cannot be both required and disabled simultaneously.
Phone fields should declare a maxlength attribute aligned with E.164 (15 digits max).
Phone fields should declare autocomplete='tel' to enable browser autofill.
If a placeholder is declared, verify it provides a meaningful phone format example.
Verify rejection of phone numbers with invalid country code (+000...). Country code 0 does not exist in E.164 — a system that accepts it may not validate country codes at all.

URL.description: If the URL field is marked as required, it must prevent empty submissions.
URL fields should use type='url' for browser-level validation and UX.
URL fields should have an accessible label for screen reader support.
URL fields must declare a 'name' attribute for form submission.
Verify that empty URL fields block submission when required.
Verify that a well-formed http:// URL is accepted.
Verify that a well-formed https:// URL is accepted.
Verify rejection of URLs without protocol (example.com without http/https).
Verify that non-http/https schemes like ftp:// are rejected. Most web applications should only accept http/https URLs; accepting ftp:// may indicate insufficient scheme validation.
The field must reject javascript: scheme URLs (XSS attack vector).
The field must reject data: URI scheme URLs (security risk).
Verify rejection of URLs with domain missing TLD (https://example without .com).
Verify rejection of URLs with consecutive dots in domain (https://example..com).
Verify acceptance of URL with domain label at boundary (63 chars = RFC max per label).
Verify rejection of URL with domain label exceeding 63 chars.
Verify that URLs with a valid port number are accepted.
Verify rejection of URLs with invalid port numbers (e.g., :99999).
Verify that URLs with path components are accepted.
Verify that URLs with query string parameters are accepted.
Verify rejection or truncation of extremely long URLs (2000+ chars).
Verify rejection of URLs containing literal spaces.
Verify rejection of URLs with invalid domain syntax (underscores, etc.).
Verify rejection of URLs containing control characters.
Verify the URL field rejects HTML injection attempts.
Test for SQL Injection vulnerability in URL field.
Verify that percent-encoded spaces (%20) in URLs are accepted.
Verify that URLs with subdomains are accepted.
If a pattern attribute is declared on the URL field, verify its regex is syntactically valid.
URL fields should declare a maxlength attribute consistent with typical browser limits.
Detect incoherence: a URL field cannot be both required and disabled simultaneously.
Verify rejection of file:// scheme URLs. Accepting file:// in a URL field can enable Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) attacks by causing the server to read local filesystem paths.

DATE.description: If the date field is marked as required, it must prevent empty submissions.
Date fields should use type='date' for native picker support and format normalization.
Date fields should have an accessible label for screen reader support.
Date fields must declare a 'name' attribute for form submission.
Date fields should declare a min attribute if there is a lower date bound constraint.
Date fields should declare a max attribute if there is an upper date bound constraint.
Verify that empty date fields block submission when required.
Verify that a well-formed ISO 8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD) is accepted.
Verify rejection of structurally invalid dates (month 13, day 45).
Verify consistent handling of slash-delimited date format (2026/12/31).
Verify rejection of locale-specific date format (DD/MM/YYYY) when ISO format is expected.
Verify rejection of full ISO 8601 timestamps when only date is expected.
Verify rejection of dates with alphanumeric month values (2026-AB-10).
Verify rejection of month values exceeding 12.
Verify rejection of invalid day values (e.g., April 31).
Verify acceptance of Feb 29 on a valid leap year.
Verify rejection of Feb 29 on a non-leap year.
Verify rejection of the null date 0000-00-00.
Verify acceptance of a historical date (1900-01-01). This is informational — the actual minimum valid date depends on the field's business context.
Test system behavior for dates before 1900-01-01. Rejection is expected only if the field declares an explicit min constraint; without it, this is informational.
Verify consistent handling of future dates (accept if no max constraint).
Verify rejection of dates with years exceeding 4-digit limits (10000-01-01).
Test for SQL Injection vulnerability in date field.
Test for Cross-Site Scripting vulnerability in date field.
Test system behavior for a birthdate indicating a minor (age < 18). Rejection is expected only if the form explicitly restricts to adults; this rule is informational for generic date fields.
Detect incoherence: a date field cannot be both required and disabled simultaneously.
Verify rejection of timezone injection in date-time strings (2026-01-01T00:00:00+99:99). Invalid timezone offsets may cause date parsing errors or silent value distortion on the server.

CHECKBOX.description: Mandatory checkboxes must have the required attribute and block submission when unchecked.
Checkboxes must have an accessible label to describe their purpose.
Checkboxes must declare an 'id' attribute for label association.
Checkboxes must declare a 'name' attribute for form submission.
Verify the field is declared as type='checkbox'.
Verify submission is blocked when a required checkbox is left unchecked.
Verify submission proceeds when a required checkbox is checked.
Verify rejection of null/empty value for required checkbox field.

Verify consistent handling of quoted string 'true' as checkbox value (type confusion). Note: for native HTML checkboxes, Playwright uses setChecked() — this test is only meaningful for custom checkbox components that accept arbitrary string values via API.

Verify consistent handling of numeric 1 as boolean (truthy type confusion). Note: for native HTML checkboxes, Playwright uses setChecked() — this test is only meaningful for custom checkbox components that accept arbitrary string values via API.

Verify rejection of numeric 2 (not a valid boolean equivalent). Note: for native HTML checkboxes, Playwright uses setChecked() — this test is only meaningful for custom checkbox components that accept arbitrary string values via API.

Verify consistent handling of 'TRUE' (uppercase string) as boolean. Note: for native HTML checkboxes, Playwright uses setChecked() — this test is only meaningful for custom checkbox components that accept arbitrary string values via API.

Verify rejection of array format [true,false] for a single checkbox. Note: for native HTML checkboxes, Playwright uses setChecked() — this test is only meaningful for custom checkbox components that accept arbitrary string values via API.
Test for SQL Injection vulnerability in checkbox related logic.
Test for Cross-Site Scripting vulnerability in checkbox value.
Detect incoherence: a required checkbox cannot be disabled.
Checkboxes should have a tabindex ≥ 0 to be keyboard navigable.
Checkboxes should declare a value attribute for meaningful form submission.
Verify that aria-checked attribute coherently matches the actual checked state.
If role attribute is present, verify it is 'checkbox' for native checkbox elements.
Test with generic SQL injection to cover blind server-side vulnerability in checkbox parameters.

RADIO.description: If a radio group is required, it must prevent submission without a selection.
Radio buttons in a group must share a 'name' attribute for group behavior.
Each radio button must have a unique 'id' attribute for label association.
The radio group should have an accessible grouping label.
Verify the field is declared as type='radio'.
The system must reject values not corresponding to any valid option (DOM tampering).
Verify rejection of null/empty value when radio group is required.
Verify that selecting a valid option allows form submission to proceed.
Verify rejection of case-varied option values (opcion_a vs OPCION_A) to prevent case-insensitive bypass.
Verify rejection of multiple simultaneous values in single-select radio group.
Verify rejection of duplicate values in radio submission.
Verify rejection of numeric values when string option values are expected.
Verify rejection of script injection embedded within a valid option value.
Test for SQL Injection vulnerability in radio value submission.
Test for Cross-Site Scripting vulnerability in radio value submission.
Detect incoherence: required radio group cannot have all options disabled.
Radio buttons should be keyboard navigable via tabindex.
Each radio button must declare a value attribute for meaningful form submission.
If role attribute is present, verify it is 'radio' for native radio elements.
Test with generic SQL injection to probe server-side radio value processing.

SELECT.description: If a select field is required, it must prevent submission without a valid selection.
Select fields must have an accessible label for screen reader support.
Select fields must declare an 'id' attribute for label association.
Select fields must declare a 'name' attribute for form submission.
Select fields must have at least one option element to be functional.
The system must reject values that do not exist in the declared dropdown options.
Verify rejection of empty/null value when select field is required.
Verify that selecting a valid option allows form submission to proceed.
Verify rejection of case-varied option values to prevent case-insensitive bypass.
Verify that deactivated/removed options are not accepted by the backend.
Verify that hidden options injected via DOM manipulation are rejected.
Verify consistent handling of multiple selected values in a single-select field.
Verify consistent handling of numeric IDs as option values.
Verify rejection of JSON object format as option value (type confusion).
Verify consistent handling of array-wrapped single option value.
Verify rejection of dependent select values that don't match parent selection.
Test for SQL Injection vulnerability in select field.
Test for Cross-Site Scripting vulnerability in select field.
Detect incoherence: a select field cannot be both required and disabled.
Select fields should be keyboard navigable via tabindex.
Select fields should have a disabled placeholder option (value='') as default.
If role attribute is present, verify it is 'listbox' or 'combobox' for select elements.
Custom select components should declare aria-expanded for accessible state communication.
Test with generic SQL injection to probe blind server-side select value processing.

TEXTAREA.description: If the textarea is marked as required, it must prevent submission when empty.
Textarea fields should have an accessible label for screen reader support.
Textarea fields must declare a 'name' attribute for form submission.
Textarea fields should declare a maxlength attribute to prevent excessive input.
Verify that empty textarea blocks submission when required.
Verify rejection of input containing only whitespace characters (spaces, newlines).
Verify rejection of textarea content below declared minimum length.
Verify acceptance of textarea content at exactly the minimum length boundary.
Verify acceptance of textarea content at exactly the maximum length boundary.
Verify rejection or truncation of textarea content exceeding maximum length.
Verify that multiline text with newlines is accepted.
Verify that HTML tags submitted in a textarea are rejected or sanitized server-side. A field that stores raw HTML without sanitization is a stored XSS vulnerability.
Test for Cross-Site Scripting vulnerability in textarea fields.
Test for SQL Injection vulnerability in textarea fields.
Verify that textarea accepts text with special characters without unjustified filtering.
Detect incoherence: a textarea cannot be both required and disabled simultaneously.
Textarea should declare rows and cols attributes for consistent rendering.
If minlength is declared, verify its value is reasonable (> 0 and < maxlength).

FILE.description: If a file input is marked as required, it must prevent submission when no file is selected.
File inputs should declare an 'accept' attribute to restrict allowed file types at browser level.
File inputs should have an accessible label for screen reader support.
File inputs must declare a 'name' attribute for form submission.
Verify that the system rejects files with unauthorized extensions.
Verify that the system rejects files exceeding the maximum allowed size.
Verify that a valid PDF file is accepted.
Verify rejection of files with double extensions used to hide malicious content (e.g., .pdf.exe).
Verify acceptance of a file at exactly the maximum allowed size.
Verify rejection of a file slightly above the maximum allowed size.
Verify rejection of files where MIME type does not match the declared extension.
Verify rejection of files simulating malware signatures (EICAR test string).
Verify rejection of filenames containing path traversal sequences (../../../etc/passwd).
If multiple file uploads are expected, the 'multiple' attribute should be declared.
Detect incoherence: a required file input cannot be disabled.

UNKNOWN.description: If the unknown field has a required attribute, verify it blocks empty submissions.
Unknown fields must declare a 'name' attribute for proper form submission.
Insert a destructive 5000-character string to test overflow and middleware failure.
Test for blind SQL Injection in fields of unknown type.
Probe for XSS vulnerabilities in generic inputs of unknown type.
Insert a standard string to infer if the field accepts text.
Insert a numeric string to infer if the field expects numbers.
Insert a JSON object as value to detect type confusion vulnerabilities.
Insert binary/null-byte sequences to test for unsafe deserialization or buffer issues.
Insert a 5000-character string to test generic overflow handling.
